question.text: What is the Consolidated Account Statement (CAS) and how frequently is it issued?

answer.text: A physical certificate of holdings issued annually by each AMC separately
A single statement covering all mutual fund holdings across AMCs, issued monthly if there are transactions and half-yearly otherwise
A statement issued only on redemption showing capital gains and losses
A quarterly portfolio disclosure statement required by SEBI

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** CAS is a consolidated statement that shows all mutual fund holdings and transactions of an investor across all AMCs in a single document. It is generated by the RTAs (CAMS and KFin Technologies). CAS is issued monthly if any financial transaction has occurred during the month, and half-yearly (September and March) even if there are no transactions.
**The Trap:** CAS is not the same as individual AMC account statements. CAS covers holdings across ALL AMCs linked to the investor's PAN. It provides a complete picture of the investor's mutual fund portfolio in one document.
**Cross-Exam Context:** CAS also includes details of demat holdings alongside mutual fund holdings, giving investors a comprehensive view of their investment portfolio. It serves as the primary record of mutual fund holdings since physical unit certificates are no longer issued.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Encourage clients to review their CAS regularly. It is the best tool for portfolio review meetings — it shows everything in one place. Help clients register their email for eCAS delivery, which is faster and more accessible than physical statements.
